title: Bitter Fluffy Jamaican Cassaba Stew
description: A hearty, weeknight-friendly stew featuring tender cassava cubes, bitter greens, and aromatic spices finished with creamy coconut milk.
instructions: Peel the cassava and cut it into 1‑inch cubes. In a bowl, toss the cubes with lime juice, salt, and pepper; let marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, allspice, thyme, and nutmeg; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the marinated cassava cubes to the pot and stir to coat them in the spices. Cook for 3 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth and coconut milk, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 15 minutes.
Add the chopped bitter greens, stir, and continue to simmer for an additional 5 minutes until the greens are tender and the stew has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed. Serve hot over steamed rice or with crusty bread.
Optional: sprinkle fresh thyme leaves on top for garnish.
